a: Constants that indicate the axis and direction to use for a perspective projection matrix.
The convention that uses a counterclockwise winding order and positions the leading pixel at the bottom-left corner of the view.
The convention that uses a clockwise winding order and positions the leading pixel at the bottom-left corner of the view.
The convention that uses a counterclockwise winding order and positions the leading pixel at the top-left corner of the view.
The convention that uses a clockwise winding order and positions the leading pixel at the top-left corner of the view.
Take control of the drawing environment and render your own content using Metal.
The sequential index for a frame in the compositor’s timeline.
A type that you use with an immersive space to display fully immersive content using Metal.
An interface for specifying the texture configurations and rendering behaviors to use with your Metal rendering engine.
Enable flexible immersive rendering by supporting progressive immersion when rendering your Metal content.
A type that configures the layer with the default texture configurations and rendering behaviors for the current device.
Declare the render quality of your textures to enable high-quality rendering.
A type that provides information on how to render content into the frame’s textures.

t: Drawing fully immersive content using Metal
a: Create a fully immersive experience in visionOS using a custom Metal-based rendering engine.
A type that represents a single frame of content, manages the pace of animations, and provides access to the drawable environment.

t: Interacting with virtual content blended with passthrough
a: Present a mixed immersion style space to draw content in a person’s surroundings, and choose how upper limbs appear with respect to rendered content.

The state of ownership for the drawable.
A drawable that’s not in use and ready for assignment to a frame.
A drawable that the compositor is currently displaying.
A drawable that’s assigned to a frame and ready to accept your drawing commands.
The target where the drawable will be displayed/used.
A drawable that is targeting the built-in display, this is what a user will see in the device.

A layout that assigns a separate texture to each rendered view.
A layout that specifies each view’s content as a slice of a single texture.
A layout that uses a single texture to store the content for all rendered views.
A type that describes the organization of the layer renderer’s textures and the relationships between those textures and the views you use for drawing.
Render quality controls the quality which drawing happens at.
The states of the layer renderer, which tell you how to proceed with drawing operations.
A state that indicates the layer no longer supports drawing operations.

Errors that can occur when configuring layers.
An error that indicates the configuration’s current layout value is invalid.
An error that indicates the system didn’t find a default layer configuration.
An error that indicates not enough frames are available for rendering.
An error that occurs when you try to enable temporal anti-aliasing but the current configuration parameters don’t support it.
An error that indicates your app requested too many frames for rendering.
An error that indicates the system doesn’t support the specified color format choice.
An error that indicates the system doesn’t support the specified color usage option.
An error that indicates the system doesn’t support the specified depth format choice.
An error that indicates the system doesn’t support the specified depth usage choice.
An error that indicates the layer doesn’t support the current pixel format for the stencil texture.
An error that indicates the depth range values aren’t in reverse-z order.
An error that indicates the near plane of the client is closer than the minimum supported distance.

An error that indicates the configuration’s render quality is unsupported. This could be because foveation is disabled or the quality is outside of the valid range of [0, 1], the error `userInfo` will contain additional information.
An error that indicates the layer doesn’t support the current pixel format for tracking areas textures.
An error that indicates the layer doesn’t support the current texture usage for tracking areas textures.
An error that indicates foveation is enabled but not supported.

t: Rendering hover effects in Metal immersive apps

Returns the transform which can be used for binocular frustum culling. A matrix to convert between the device coordinate space to normalized device coordinate space. This should be acquired after query frame via [cp_layer_renderer_query_next_frame](/documentation/compositorservices/cp_layer_renderer_query_next_frame) and before calling into [endSubmission()](/documentation/compositorservices/layerrenderer/frame/endsubmission()). Renderer should not utilize this transform for actual rendering output.

Returns the transform which can be used for monocular frustum culling. A matrix to convert between the device coordinate space to normalized device coordinate space. This should be acquired after query frame via [cp_layer_renderer_query_next_frame](/documentation/compositorservices/cp_layer_renderer_query_next_frame) and before calling into [endSubmission()](/documentation/compositorservices/layerrenderer/frame/endsubmission()). Renderer should not utilize this transform for actual rendering output.
